verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is a multipass transmembrane inositol-1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor residing exclusively in the endoplasmic reticulum membrane. No documented plasma-membrane isoform, no ectodomain shedding, no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering, no PM minority pool, no surface-directed therapeutics. Ruled out: cell-state-induced translocation (no cancer/apoptosis/activation surface-display literature); tissue-restricted surface (ER-exclusive in all tissues); lysosomal exocytosis (ER resident, not lysosomal); dual localization (no PM cycling documented); stable surface attachment (not secreted, not tethered to PM partner).
